title: Agent Queue Task
description: A decision-ready unit of work written by a planner for a worker agent. A task is not queueable unless it carries context, a spec, acceptance criteria, a verification plan, and scope boundaries. The schema is deliberately opinionated toward agent handoff; swap it via agent-queue.config.json if your work has a different shape.

target.description: Optional label for the thing this task operates on: a project, repo, dataset, or lane. Tasks that share a target are claimed one-at-a-time so unrelated targets run in parallel. Omit it for no concurrency limit.

lane.description: autonomous: a worker may claim without approval. needs_owner: requires owner_approval.approved before claim.

context.description: The handoff brief. The worker starts cold: cover why this task exists, what is already known (current behavior, root cause if diagnosed, what was tried or ruled out), relevant conventions or constraints, and any gotchas. A competent worker with zero prior context should be able to start without asking a question.

spec.description: What to do, concretely: the change in plain language, including where it goes and what pattern to follow.

source_references.description: References that ground the task: where the change happens, where the pattern to follow lives, where the problem shows up. These replace the worker's discovery pass. For code this is file/line refs; for other work it can be a document, dataset, or URL plus a substantive note.

setup_notes.description: How to get set up for this task when non-obvious: install/build/run commands, ports, env vars, profile names (never secret values).

verification.description: Proof plan. Each entry is an exact command (or concrete manual check) the worker must run and the specific expected outcome - not 'works' or 'passes'.

out_of_scope.description: Explicit non-goals. Workers must not expand scope past this line; new findings become follow_ups, not extra work. At least one entry - every task has an adjacent thing it should NOT touch.

follow_ups.description: Out-of-scope findings the worker surfaced instead of acting on. The planner triages these into new tasks.
